parsed_review.summary: CP00-666 opens RP22 External Integrations I as a descriptor-only, no-write foundation for IntegrationConnection, OAuthCredentialRef, SyncJob, ExternalMessage, ESignRequest, and WebhookEvent. Static read-only inspection confirms the implementation backs every closeout claim: registry/service/validators are pure frozen descriptor builders with no network, credential, or product-state writes; the 40+ no-write attestation flags are bidirectionally enforced; all external API/OAuth/sync/message/e-sign/webhook/credential/permission/audit/UI/Hermes runtime boundaries are closed and asserted; Claude is held to read-only non-final-approver; and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling. production_ready and runtime_ready are both false — the pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust. Deliverable distribution resolves to 108/8/16/7/3/8=150 matching the baseline, the hardened review preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, Read/Grep/Glob only), and no secrets are leaked. Provenance note: the 22 validation commands are reported exit-0 in command-evidence.json and were NOT independently re-executed under this read-only review; the verdict rests on static coherence plus evidence consistency. Two P3 informational findings (intentional byte-identical core/external contract files; benign --write-gated contract-generation path) are non-blocking. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; pack closeout for the CP00-666 descriptor goal is supported.

findings.title: Core and external contract files are byte-identical projections (by design)
findings.details: contracts/integrations-core-contract.json and contracts/external-integrations-i-contract.json are byte-for-byte identical (both 4616 lines, identical head and tail). This is intentional: scripts/validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs:53-56 writes the same createExternalIntegrationsICoreContractProjection() output to both paths, and packages/integrations-core/test/model.test.js:93-101 asserts both validate against the one projection. Not a correctness or security issue, but the two distinct filenames (core vs external) imply differentiated scope that does not yet exist.
findings.recommendation: Acknowledge as a deliberate foundation choice. When a downstream pack differentiates core-owned vs external-integration scope, ensure the two contract files are allowed to diverge rather than silently assumed identical by the projection-equality test.

findings.title: Validator contains a --write-gated contract-generation write path; confirmed out of scope of the no-write attestation
findings.details: The pack's no-write/no-real-data attestation is scoped to product/runtime state (DB rows, object storage, credentials, external APIs, audit/permission writes, Hermes runtime receipts), not to all filesystem writes. The one filesystem write path in the pack is the --write-gated regeneration of the two contract descriptor JSON files in scripts/validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs:53-56 (writeJson). Verified benign: it emits only the deterministic descriptor projection (no real or secret data) and is skipped during normal validation runs. Documented here for review transparency since 'no-write' could be misread as 'no filesystem writes at all'.
findings.recommendation: No change required. Keep the contract-generation write strictly gated behind --write and limited to the two descriptor contract files so it remains outside the product-state no-write boundary.
